Acta v0.2.0-pilot record · Generated from accepted canonical state (blueprints/tag-filter.md) after explicit acceptance (chat 2026-07-23 — Gate B candidate pasted and confirmed; Gate A accepted 2026-07-22) · Never generated from browser state.

decision record · ACCEPTED

Tag-filter change blueprint — spec and plan accepted

The tag-filter specification (Gate A) and its five-slice implementation plan (Gate B) are both accepted: pure functional core, DOM-last dependency direction, twelve planned assertions, single-revert rollback.

Accepted gates

Gate A — specification

Single ?tag= filter over the static skills catalog; pure filterByTag testable in Node; chips as filter links; full catalog without JavaScript; no network, no multi-tag, no persistence.

Rationale: Scope and non-goals mirror the interview frontier; all six criteria are mechanically checkable.. Accepted via chat 2026-07-22 — explicit confirmation of the pasted candidate.

Gate B — implementation plan

approve-plan — Approve this plan as the build contract for the tag-filter change?

Rationale: Slices are independently green, the flow direction matches the accepted code-path decision, rollback is one revert.. Accepted via chat 2026-07-23 — explicit confirmation of the pasted candidate.

5sliceseach independently green
3files touchedcatalog · url-tag · app
12planned assertionsnode --test
1 commitrollback unitrevert restores static catalog

Slice plan

Five vertical slices on the accepted functional-core shape; nothing is user-visible until slice 4, and every slice leaves node --test green.

SliceDeliversTouches
Slice 1Pure filterByTag with its own assertions
filterByTag(skills, tag) lands as a plain importable function: null tag returns the full list, unknown tags return empty, matches filter by inclusion.		src/catalog.mjstest/catalog.test.mjs
Slice 2Pure parseTagFromSearch
parseTagFromSearch(search) reads the single ?tag= parameter via URLSearchParams and returns the tag or null; no DOM involved.		src/url-tag.mjstest/url-tag.test.mjs
Slice 3Static chip markup in the no-JS catalog
Every skill row renders its tags as plain ?tag= links so the page is complete and navigable before any script runs.		index.html
Slice 4Browser glue: read URL, filter, replaceChildren
app.mjs wires location.search through the two pure functions and re-renders the list; the empty state from interface-directions renders for unknown tags.		src/app.mjs
Slice 5Back/forward wiring and final sweep
popstate re-runs the same glue; acceptance criteria walked end to end at 1440 and 320, with and without JavaScript.		src/app.mjsqa notes

Data flow

One direction of dependency: the DOM depends on pure functions, never the reverse. The dark node is the only stateful surface.

Solid = call path on every navigation. Dashed = the browser-only wiring added in slice 4; nothing to the left of it knows the DOM exists.

Riskiest code surface

The only tricky surface: the browser glue must fall back to the full catalog when the URL has no tag, without double-rendering on load.

src/app.mjs (planned, slice 4)
import { filterByTag } from "./catalog.mjs";
import { parseTagFromSearch } from "./url-tag.mjs";

function renderFromUrl() {
  const tag = parseTagFromSearch(location.search);
  const visible = filterByTag(visibleSkills(), tag);
  // Empty list + known tag = accepted empty state, not a blank <ul>
  replaceChildren(list, visible.length ? rows(visible) : emptyState(tag));
}

window.addEventListener("popstate", renderFromUrl);
renderFromUrl();

Risks, tests, rollback

RiskLikelihoodMitigation
Unknown ?tag= values render a blank list instead of the accepted empty statemediumSlice 4 renders emptyState(tag) whenever the filtered list is empty; acceptance criterion 3 checks it explicitly.
Chip links fight the JS re-render and cause a full page reloadmediumChips stay plain links; slice 5 intercepts clicks only when history.pushState is available, so no-JS behavior is untouched.
visibleSkills() ordering changes under filteringlowfilterByTag preserves input order by construction; a test asserts stable ordering.

Proof

  • filterByTag: null tag, matching tag, unknown tag, order preservation (4 assertions)
  • parseTagFromSearch: missing, present, duplicate parameters (3 assertions)
  • Static markup: every tag chip resolves to a real catalog tag (2 assertions)
  • Glue: unknown tag renders the empty state, popstate re-renders (3 assertions)

Rollout, then rollback

  • Slices land behind nothing — each is inert until slice 4 wires the glue
  • Slice 4 ships last, after 1–3 are green in CI
  • Manual sweep at 1440/320, JS on and off, before calling it done
Rollback
  • Single revert of the slice-4 commit restores the static catalog exactly
  • Slices 1–3 are pure additions and can stay in place harmlessly

Unresolved at acceptance

  • whether slice 5 intercepts chip clicks or lets them navigate (decided during build)

Sources

  1. [E1] spec.md — accepted at Gate A
  2. [E2] src/catalog.mjs — visibleSkills() idiom
  3. [E3] three-code-paths record — functional core, thin shell

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
